description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly white coloration, interspersed with brown and tan mineral veining and mottling. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural indentations, crevices, and a few slightly smoother patches. The stone appears to be composed of a dense, crystalline material, likely quartz or marble, with some weathered areas showing subtle discoloration and mineral deposits. Its form is blocky but asymmetrical, with no sharp edges, and it has a substantial, solid appearance. The coloration and texture suggest it has been exposed to the elements, possibly in an urban park or landscaped area. The stone's mass and size make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative barrier in public spaces.
description: A rough, blocky white stone with brown veining and mottling, featuring an irregular, weathered surface. Its solid, dense appearance makes it a natural urban barrier or decorative element.
description_view_0: The front view shows a broad, uneven face with prominent brown veining and mottling, and a deep indentation on the upper surface.
description_view_1: This side reveals a more vertical face with lighter coloration and subtle brown streaks, with less pronounced surface features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flatter, whiter surface with faint horizontal striations and minimal brown coloration.
description_view_3: This angle highlights a rounded, lighter side with a small cavity and gentle brown shading, giving a softer appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with brown and tan mineral streaks, creating a natural, non-repetitive pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly discolored, with visible mineral deposits and minor surface pitting indicating moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped settings.
